imgAlt: Autumn Glow Batik Suit Set styled with gold jhumkas
label: The Golden Hour
title: Let the dupatta do the work
desc: Drape it loosely over one shoulder, let it fall naturally. Add a single gold jhumka and flat kolhapuris — the print is loud enough, everything else stays quiet.
jewelry: Single gold jhumka (one ear), thin gold bangles
accessories: Flat tan kolhapuris, minimal potli or no bag

imgAlt: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set Blue styled minimally
label: The Quiet Blue
title: When the neckline does the talking
desc: The hand-stitched neckline is the detail — don't compete with it. Wear oxidised silver studs, leave your hair open, and carry nothing but a clutch.
jewelry: Small oxidised silver studs, a single thin silver ring
productName: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set – Blue

imgAlt: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set Wine – outdoor styled
label: The Deep Wine
title: Dark tones need breathing room
desc: With wine, you resist the urge to layer. No stole, no dupatta piled on. Wear it clean, add a small antique gold maang teeka if it's an occasion — or nothing at all if it isn't.
jewelry: Antique gold maang teeka, thin gold chain
productName: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set – Wine

imgAlt: Batik Alia Anarkali styled with statement earrings
label: The Floor Length
moment: When the silhouette is the accessory
title: An anarkali needs one statement, not five
desc: The flare speaks. Pull your hair back in a low bun, wear one pair of long chandelier earrings, and let the hem do the rest. Block heels keep it grounded without killing the drama.
jewelry: Long chandelier earrings (gold or oxidised), no other jewellery
accessories: Block heel sandals, small potli bag in contrast fabric

imgAlt: Silver Rain Suit Set styled for evening
label: The Shimmer
title: How to wear shimmer without overdoing it
desc: The lurex weave already glints — don't stack it with heavy stones. Small pearl drops in the ears, hair half-up, and silver heeled mules. The outfit shimmers; you stay effortless.
jewelry: Small freshwater pearl drop earrings, thin silver bangles
accessories: Metallic silver heeled mules, minimal micro bag

imgAlt: Batik Lint Noodle Strap Suit Set with layered look
label: The Noodle Strap
title: Style it bare, or layer it with a shrug
desc: Two ways to wear this: pure noodle-strap for summer heat — minimal silver earrings, flat sandals, done. Or layer a sheer white cotton dupatta as a wrap for evenings. Both work.
jewelry: Tiny silver hoop earrings or thin coin drops
accessories: Flat leather sandals or platform wedges, tote bag
productName: Batik Lint Noodle Strap Suit Set

imgAlt: Batik Olive Bloom Set with earthy accessories
label: The Olive Edit
title: Pair earth tones with earth textures
desc: Olive wants jute, bamboo, and raw wood — not plastic or shine. A woven jute tote, beaded tassel earrings, and brown kolhapuris complete this look without a single mirror.
jewelry: Beaded tassel earrings (terracotta or amber tones), wooden bangle
accessories: Jute tote bag, brown flat kolhapuris

imgAlt: Batik Scarlet Garden Set styled with gold
label: The Scarlet Garden
title: One gold piece — that's the rule
desc: Scarlet red is already your focal point. Pick one gold piece: a thick gold bangle, a jhumka, or a chain. Not all three. Braid your hair back and let the batik print bloom.
jewelry: One thick gold bangle OR gold jhumkas (not both), no neck piece
accessories: Gold or tan juttis, small embroidered clutch

imgAlt: Batik Silver Stripe Co-Ord styled as separates
label: The Co-Ord Play
moment: Wear it together, or break it apart
title: A co-ord is two pieces — use that
desc: Wear the set together for zero-effort dressing. Or pair just the top with denim and sneakers when you want the print without the full look. The stripe makes both versions work.
jewelry: Delicate layered chains (2–3 thin chains at different lengths)
accessories: White sneakers for casual; block sandals for dressy

imgAlt: Batik Nayra Suit Set styled with dupatta as a belt
label: The Nayra Knot
title: Knot the dupatta — don't just drape it
desc: Fold the dupatta lengthwise into a thin strip and tie it loosely at the waist — it becomes a sash. Changes the silhouette entirely. Add chunky silver oxidised earrings and you're done.
jewelry: Chunky oxidised silver jhumkas, silver toe ring
accessories: Flat embroidered juttis, no bag or a potli

imgAlt: Batik Lurex Weave Dress styled with minimal accessories
label: The Dress Edit
title: A dress is a complete thought — don't interrupt it
desc: The lurex weave dress works best alone. No dupatta, no stole. Stack 2–3 thin gold rings on your fingers, wear gold kitten heels, and carry a tiny box clutch. Done in 5 minutes.
jewelry: Stacked thin gold rings (fingers), small gold studs
accessories: Gold kitten heels, small structured box clutch

imgAlt: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set Lavender styled softly
label: The Lavender Mood
title: Match the mood of the colour
desc: Lavender is a soft colour — style it softly. Rose-gold jewellery instead of bright gold. A floral printed potli bag. Hair in a low braid with a few loose pieces. Nothing sharp or heavy.
jewelry: Rose-gold small hoops or floral stud earrings, rose-gold delicate bracelet
accessories: Floral printed potli, soft suede block sandals in blush
productName: Batik Crazy In Love Suit Set – Lavender

imgAlt: Batik Fit and Flare Dress Lavender styled for movement
label: The Flare
moment: Dress for how it moves, not just how it looks
title: A fit-and-flare wants to be walked in
desc: Choose a heel that makes the flare bounce — a kitten heel or a block heel. Silver anklet on one ankle. Minimal earrings. Let the dress move and photograph the hem, not just the face.
jewelry: Silver anklet (one ankle), tiny silver studs
accessories: Block heel mules (nude or silver), embroidered sling bag
